topic: Restate - durable execution engine for distributed applications

sources.title: Restate Official Website
keyInsights: Lightweight runtime converting services into durable processes
Built by Apache Flink creators
<100ms p99 latency for 10-step workflows
13K workflows/sec on 3-node cluster
Supports Lambda, Cloud Run, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel

sources.title: Restate Documentation Hub
keyInsights: Four core capabilities: durable execution, built-in state, reliable communication, time-based coordination
SDKs for TypeScript, Java, Kotlin, Python, Go, Rust
Primary use cases: AI agents, workflows, microservice orchestration, event processing

sources.title: Restate GitHub Repository
keyInsights: Written in Rust (99.2% of codebase)
3,700+ stars, 143 forks, 3,661 commits
65 releases, latest v1.6.2 (Feb 2026)
Supports Homebrew, npm, Docker, binary installation

sources.title: Durable Building Blocks
keyInsights: ctx.run() wraps non-deterministic operations for journaling
State persistence via get/set/clear on K/V store
Handlers consume no resources during sleep
Durable timers resume at exactly the right time across restarts

sources.title: Service Types Documentation
keyInsights: Three service types: Service, Virtual Object, Workflow
Virtual Objects enforce single-writer per key with concurrent readers
Workflows execute exactly once per ID with concurrent signal handlers
State retained indefinitely for Virtual Objects

sources.title: Invocation Model
keyInsights: HTTP, typed client, and Kafka invocation methods
Idempotency keys for deduplication
Invocation attachment for monitoring ongoing requests
Cancellation support with saga rollback

sources.title: Durable Execution Concept
keyInsights: Journal tracks every step; replay skips completed steps
Protects against infrastructure failures, API timeouts, duplicates, network issues
State updates automatically journaled alongside execution
Single-writer guarantee prevents concurrent state modifications

sources.title: Building a Modern Durable Execution Engine from First Principles
keyInsights: Bifrost: distributed replicated log as primary durability mechanism
3ms median step latency at low load
94,286 actions/sec at high load (1200 clients)
Partitioned scale-out model avoids cross-shard coordination
RocksDB indexes with S3 snapshots for tiered storage
Epoch-based leader failover prevents split-brain

sources.title: Why We Built Restate
keyInsights: Founded by Apache Flink creators (Stephan Ewen, Till, Igal)
Bridges RPC simplicity with event-driven reliability
Log-as-ground-truth eliminates distributed coordination
Designed for FaaS compatibility from the start

sources.title: TypeScript SDK Overview
keyInsights: Node.js >= v20, Bun, or Deno required
Service, Object, Workflow constructors with typed contexts
Shared handlers for concurrent read access
HTTP/2 server on port 9080 by default

sources.title: Java/Kotlin SDK Overview
keyInsights: JDK >= 17 required
Annotation-based service definition (@Service, @VirtualObject, @Workflow)
Annotation processor required for code generation
Spring Boot starters available

sources.title: Python SDK Overview
keyInsights: Python >= 3.11 required
Decorator-based handler definition
Async-first API design
Workflow main handler via @workflow.main()

sources.title: Go SDK Overview
keyInsights: Go >= 1.21 required
Struct-based handler definition with restate.Reflect()
Protobuf code generation available
Mock package for testing

sources.title: Rust SDK Repository
keyInsights: v0.9.0, actively developing (may break across releases)
Trait-based service definition with #[restate_sdk::service] macro
HttpServer and LambdaEndpoint deployment
Testcontainers support

sources.title: Journaling Results (TypeScript)
keyInsights: ctx.run wraps non-deterministic operations; results stored in execution log
Cannot invoke Restate context within ctx.run
Deterministic alternatives: ctx.rand.uuidv4(), ctx.rand.random(), ctx.date.now()
Default suspension after 1 minute of inactivity

sources.title: Awakeables Documentation
keyInsights: Durable distributed primitives for external event coordination
Create with ctx.awakeable(), resolve/reject via SDK or HTTP API
Handler pauses and survives failures while waiting
Exclusive handlers queue other calls during awakeable waits

sources.title: Service Communication (TypeScript)
keyInsights: Three patterns: request-response, one-way, delayed messages
Virtual Object messages execute serially by arrival order per key
Idempotency keys for cross-handler deduplication
Deadlock risk with circular exclusive handler calls

sources.title: State Management (TypeScript)
keyInsights: K/V state scoped per object key, retained indefinitely
Eager (default) vs lazy state loading modes
Exclusive handlers read/write; shared handlers read-only
Typed state schemas for type safety

sources.title: Serving Configuration (TypeScript)
keyInsights: Node.js HTTP/2, Lambda, Bun, Deno, Cloudflare Workers runtimes
Cloudflare Workers require minify=false and --use-http1.1
Request identity validation with ED25519 keys
Custom endpoint handler for advanced HTTP configuration

sources.title: Serialization (TypeScript)
keyInsights: JSON default serialization for journal, state, network data
Zod (Standard Schema) integration for input/output validation
Custom Serde interface for binary or other formats
Clients must specify custom serializers explicitly

sources.title: Error Handling (TypeScript)
keyInsights: Infinite retries with exponential backoff by default
TerminalError halts retry cycle and propagates to callers
asTerminalError option for automatic error mapping
Compensation needed when throwing after successful side effects

sources.title: Durable Timers (TypeScript)
keyInsights: Sleep for months or years with accurate resume after failures
Serverless: handler suspended during sleep to save resources
Delayed messages preferred over sleep+send pattern
orTimeout() for bounded operations

sources.title: Testing Restate Services
keyInsights: Testcontainers-based integration testing against real Restate server
stateOf() for state inspection and manipulation in tests
Type-safe state operations with generics
Integration testing preferred over context mocking

sources.title: Architecture Reference
keyInsights: Four layers: Ingress, Bifrost (durable log), Partition Processor, Control Plane
Quorum replication for write commits
Segmented virtual logs for efficient reconfiguration
RocksDB derivative state rebuilt from log on recovery
Raft consensus for cluster metadata
Role-based node specialization (metadata, ingress, log, worker)

sources.title: SQL Introspection API
keyInsights: SQL queryable tables for debugging and monitoring
sys_invocation tracks lifecycle with 8 status values
sys_journal records individual operations per invocation
state table exposes K/V state with binary support

sources.title: Versioning and Service Updates
keyInsights: Immutable deployments: requests start and end on same version
FaaS platforms provide natural immutable versioning
Kubernetes Operator automates registration and draining
Journal mismatches (RT0016) from reordering SDK operations
Pause/resume for fixing in-flight invocations

sources.title: Security Documentation
keyInsights: ED25519 request identity cryptographic verification
Private services restrict HTTP ingress access
Client-side journal encryption available
Network isolation: only Restate needs service access

sources.title: Upgrade Guide
keyInsights: SemVer with mandatory incremental minor version upgrades
Rollback safe from x.y.0 to latest x.(y-1).z
SDKs version independently from server
Backup data before any upgrade

sources.title: Server Configuration
keyInsights: Configuration via TOML file, env vars (RESTATE_ prefix), or CLI args
--dump-config outputs effective configuration
SIGUSR1 signal for live config dump
Server roles: worker, admin, metadata-server, log-server, http-ingress

sources.title: Saga Pattern Guide
keyInsights: Compensating actions registered after each successful step
Reverse-order execution on terminal error
Two-phase APIs and idempotency keys for safe retries
Cancellation support with consistent unwinding

sources.title: Durable Webhooks Guide
keyInsights: Any handler serves as durable webhook endpoint
Exactly-once delivery with automatic deduplication
Guaranteed completion across failures
Stripe payment example demonstrates practical usage

sources.title: Request Lifecycle Guide
keyInsights: 7-step lifecycle: persist, connect, invoke, track, detect failure, replay, complete
HTTP/2 bidirectional stream between server and service
Inter-service calls route through Restate, not direct
HTTP/1.1 fallback with suspension at blocking points

sources.title: Error Handling Guide
keyInsights: Retry policies configurable at invocation, run-block, and action levels
on-max-attempts: pause or kill invocation
Dead-letter queue pattern for failed invocations
Inactivity timeout (1min) and abort timeout (10min) defaults

sources.title: Parallelizing Work Guide
keyInsights: Durable distributed promises for fan-out/fan-in
Subtask handlers on serverless for auto-scaling
Coordinator suspends during parallel execution
Promise combinators for result aggregation

sources.title: Rate Limiting Guide
keyInsights: Token bucket via Virtual Objects
wait(), allow(), reserve(), setRate() methods
Durable timers for token refills
Cancelable reservations

sources.title: Cron Jobs Guide
keyInsights: Custom scheduler using Virtual Objects and durable timers
Standard cron expressions
Automatic rescheduling after each execution
Job inspection and cancellation via API

sources.title: Local Development Guide
keyInsights: restate up for quick local dev server
UI at localhost:9070 for debugging
restate invocation list/cancel/kill for management
Remove restate-data directory for clean slate

sources.title: Quickstart Guide
keyInsights: 5-step setup: install, select SDK, get template, run, register
curl testing via localhost:8080
Templates available for all languages
Durable execution demonstrated from first request

sources.title: Remote Control for Agents (Blog)
keyInsights: Cancel, pause, resume, restart capabilities for in-flight requests
Every request gets persistent invocation ID
Programmatic control via HTTP, UI, or CLI
Restart from specific step preserving expensive prior work

sources.title: Restate Blog Index
keyInsights: Active blog with 2-3 posts per month
AI agent integration posts (Langfuse, Pydantic AI, OpenAI, Google ADK)
Architecture deep dives and release announcements
SOC 2, Enterprise SSO, HIPAA compliance (Feb 2026)

sources.title: Durable Agents Tour
keyInsights: Weather agent example across 6 SDK implementations
Demonstrates journal-based recovery for AI agents
Prevents duplicate LLM calls and tool executions
Observable execution traces in Restate UI

sources.title: Use Cases Page
keyInsights: Financial workflows, AI agents, workflow orchestration, event-driven architecture
Replacing Celery for distributed task orchestration
Kafka integration for long-running workflows
Customer testimonials across diverse industries

sources.title: Restate Examples Repository
keyInsights: 25+ patterns across all SDKs
End-to-end apps: food ordering, chat bot, RAG pipeline
Templates for all languages
AWS Lambda CDK integration examples

sources.title: TypeScript SDK Repository
keyInsights: v1.11.1 (March 2026), primary SDK
Requires Node.js >= v20.19
SDK v1.6+ required for Restate Server 1.5-1.6
SDKs <= 1.4 deprecated and cannot be registered

sources.title: Java/Kotlin SDK Repository
keyInsights: Spring Boot starters (Java and Kotlin)
Kotlin coroutines support
OpenTelemetry and Log4j2 integration

sources.title: Go SDK Repository
keyInsights: Ingress SDK for external calls
Mock package for testing
Protobuf code generation

sources.title: Python SDK Repository
keyInsights: Configurable inactivity_timeout, abort_timeout, journal_retention
invocation_retry_policy support (Restate 1.4+)
92.6% Python, 6.5% Rust codebase

sources.title: XState-Restate Integration
keyInsights: Durable state machines on Restate runtime
v0.5.1 (March 2026), 9 releases
TypeScript-based (98%)
41 stars, active development

sources.title: Guides Index
keyInsights: Recipes: cron, webhooks, parallelism, rate limiting, sagas
Dev guides: databases, error handling, request lifecycle
Deploy guides: K8s Helm, K8s Operator, Docker cluster, multi-node scaling
Integrations: Encore, Kafka, XState

sources.title: Java SDK Service Communication
keyInsights: Auto-generated typed clients for services
Duration-based delayed messaging
attach() and cancel() for invocation management
Same deadlock constraints as TypeScript SDK

sources.title: Go SDK Service Communication
keyInsights: WithDelay() for delayed messages
WithIdempotencyKey() for cross-handler dedup
CancelInvocation() for stopping running handlers
Workflow run handlers execute once per ID

sources.title: Python SDK Service Communication
keyInsights: service_call/object_call/workflow_call for request-response
service_send/object_send/workflow_send for one-way
send_delay parameter with timedelta
Generic calls for dynamic service names

sources.title: Go SDK State Management
keyInsights: restate.Get[T](), restate.Set(), restate.Clear(), restate.ClearAll()
restate.Keys() for listing all state keys
Eager vs lazy state loading
Shared handlers restricted to read-only
